title: Israeli Soldiers and Settlers Kill 11 Palestinians Across Gaza and West Bank
detail: At least 11 Palestinians were killed across Gaza and the occupied West Bank on Tuesday, April 21, according to Palestinian officials and local media. In Gaza: three killed near Khan Younis — including a recently married man — by Israeli fire; one man killed in a drone strike near the Sheikh Nasser neighbourhood east of Khan Younis; Abdullah Dawas, a child, died from head injuries sustained 10 days earlier near al-Fakhoura clinic in Jabalia; one person killed at the Dawla roundabout in Zeitoun, Gaza City; and one woman killed when Israeli naval forces shelled displaced family tents northwest of Beit Lahiya. Separately, the IDF confirmed the killing of Hamas operative Anas Khaled Safi in central Gaza, saying he was planning an imminent attack on troops; a second operative, Ayman Housna (who manufactured Hamas weapons), was killed in central Gaza the previous day. Since the October 2025 ceasefire, at least 777 Palestinians in Gaza have been killed, bringing the cumulative death toll since October 7, 2023 to 72,553.

media.caption: Mourners at Nasser Hospital in Khan Younis after Israeli strikes killed Palestinians across Gaza on April 21, 2026

title: Israeli Settlers Kill 14-Year-Old Student and 32-Year-Old Man in Attack on al-Mughayyir Boys School
detail: Israeli settlers attacked al-Mughayyir Boys School east of Ramallah in the occupied West Bank on April 21, killing two Palestinians: Aws Hamdi al-Na'san, a 14-year-old student, and Jihad Marzouq Abu Na'im, 32. Their parents mourned at a Ramallah hospital as the village documented the killings. Separately, Mohammad Majdi al-Jaabari, 16, was killed after being struck by a vehicle in a ministerial security convoy near Kiryat Arba settlement in Hebron; and a 49-year-old woman died from injuries sustained during an earlier Israeli forces raid on Jenin refugee camp. Al-Mughayyir village, northeast of Ramallah, has been repeatedly targeted by settlers from nearby outposts. The attack is among the deadliest single-day settler incidents in the West Bank in 2026 and adds to the documented surge: settlers have killed 8 Palestinians in the West Bank in the first quarter of 2026 and settler violence now accounts for 75% of all displacement recorded this year.

media.caption: Family of Jihad Marzouq Abu Na'im mourning at a Ramallah hospital after he was killed in settler attack on al-Mughayyir, April 21, 2026

title: Israel Marks 78th Independence Day (Yom HaAtzmaut); Palestinians Barred from Official Ceremonies
detail: Israel's Yom HaZikaron (Memorial Day) concluded at nightfall on April 21, 2026 as Yom HaAtzmaut — Israel's 78th Independence Day — began with the ceremonial torchlight ceremony at Mount Herzl in Jerusalem. Sirens rang across Israel marking the deaths of 25,648 soldiers and terror victims since 1948. Palestinians were notably excluded from official state ceremonies. Al Jazeera documented Palestinian residents being turned away from memorial events, emphasising the chasm in how Israeli and Palestinian communities experience the same dates: for Palestinians, the two days carry the memory of the Nakba, the displacement of 700,000–750,000 Palestinians in 1948, whose 78th anniversary falls on May 15. The joint Israeli-Palestinian Memorial Ceremony, organized by Combatants for Peace and the Parents Circle-Families Forum, took place separately from official state events. The juxtaposition between official celebrations and ongoing ceasefire violations drew international commentary, with 777 Palestinians killed in Gaza since the October 2025 ceasefire.

media.caption: Palestinians not welcome as Israel marks Yom HaZikaron and transitions to 78th Independence Day
